title: 7 Herbal Essential Oils For Upset Stomach
intro_desc: If you're dealing with an upset stomach, herbal essential oils can be a great way to find relief. These oils are extracted from plants and contain active compounds that help to soothe your stomach and reduce discomfort. For example, Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm nausea and reduce stomach pain. Foeniculum vulgare, or fennel, is another herb that's often used to ease digestive issues, as it can help to relax the muscles in your stomach and improve digestion. Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe stomach ulcers and reduce inflammation.  Herbal teas made from these herbs can be incredibly beneficial for your life. Drinking a warm cup of ginger tea, for instance, can help to calm your stomach and provide relief from nausea. Fennel tea can help to improve digestion and reduce bloating, making it a great option if you're experiencing discomfort after eating. Licorice root tea can help to soothe stomach ulcers and reduce inflammation, providing long-term relief from digestive issues. By incorporating herbal teas into your daily routine, you can find relief from digestive issues and enjoy a better quality of life. You can also use these essential oils in a diffuser or apply them topically (with a carrier oil) to get relief.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for upset stomach

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains a compound called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties. Gingerol helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, soothing upset stomachs and relieving nausea. Another compound, shogaol, has a similar effect, providing quick relief from stomach discomfort. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of gingerol and shogaol also help to protect the stomach lining from damage, reducing the risk of ulcers. Regular use of Zingiber officinale essential oils may help to alleviate stomach issues and promote overall digestive health.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Zingiber officinale roots, clean and dry them.
Crush 1/2 cup of the roots into small pieces with a mortar and pestle.
Steep the crushed roots in 2 cups of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) for 2 weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container. Discard the solids.
Store the Zingiber officinale essential oil in a dark glass bottle and use 5-7 drops as needed to soothe an upset stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils contains a high concentration of bioactive compounds like limonene, anethole, and fenchone. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe and calm the digestive system. The essential oils' ability to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines can provide relief from cramps and spasms that often accompany an upset stomach. Additionally, the oils' antimicrobial properties can help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections that may be contributing to the stomach upset. By reducing inflammation and calming the digestive system, Foeniculum vulgare essential oils can help to alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal essential oils for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Foeniculum vulgare seeds and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil).
Place the seeds in a clean glass jar and cover with the carrier oil. Leave it for 2 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the seeds.
Add 10 drops of vitamin E oil to the extracted oil to preserve it and prevent spoilage.
Store the Foeniculum vulgare essential oil in a cool, dark place and use it as needed for upset stomach relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the stomach. It also contains triterpenoid saponins, which have a soothing effect on the digestive system. The antispasmodic properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils help relax the muscles in the stomach, relieving cramps and discomfort. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause stomach upset. By reducing inflammation and soothing the digestive system, Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils can provide relief from upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root and a clean glass jar with a lid.
Place 1 tablespoon of dried root into a coffee filter or cheesecloth.
Heat 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba) in a saucepan over low heat.
Carefully pour the heated oil over the root in the filter, then seal the jar and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass jar and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains menthol, menthone, and limonene as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have a calming effect on the digestive system and help to reduce inflammation in the stomach. Menthol, in particular, has a soothing action on the stomach lining, which can help to alleviate nausea and vomiting. Limonene has a relaxing effect on the muscles in the digestive tract, which can help to ease stomach cramps and spasms. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the digestive muscles, Mentha x piperita essential oils can provide relief from an upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather materials: fresh Mentha x piperita leaves (peppermint), carrier oil (coconut or olive oil), a clean glass jar, a cheese cloth or coffee filter, and a dark bottle for storage.
Wash the peppermint leaves with 1 cup of water to remove dirt and impurities. Pat dry the leaves with a paper towel.
Fill the glass jar with 2 cups of peppermint leaves and pour 1 cup of carrier oil over the leaves.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar daily. Strain the mixture through the cheese cloth or coffee filter into a bowl.

Transfer the extracted oil to the dark bottle, discard the solids, and label the bottle. Use 5-7 drops of the essential oil mixed with 1 tablespoon of carrier oil for a soothing remedy for an upset stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cuminum cyminum essential oils contains a compound called cuminaldehyde, which is responsible for its anti-inflammatory and digestive properties. Cuminaldehyde helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, making it easier for the stomach to digest food. This oil also contains a compound called limonene, which has natural carminative properties, helping to eliminate gas and reduce bloating. The antispasmodic properties of cuminaldehyde help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and discomfort. By reducing inflammation, eliminating gas, and relaxing the muscles, Cuminum cyminum essential oils can provide relief for an upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Cuminum cyminum herbal essential oils for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cuminum cyminum seeds, a glass jar, a coffee filter, and a carrier oil like coconut oil.
Dry the Cuminum cyminum seeds in a low oven (150°F) for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the ground powder with 8 ounces (1 cup) of carrier oil in the glass jar. Stir well.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily. Strain the mixture through a coffee filter and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains a compound called curcumin, which is a powerful anti-inflammatory agent. This property helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, making it a potential solution for upset stomach. Curcumin also has antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals that can damage stomach lining and cause pain. Additionally, Curcuma longa essential oils contain a compound called demethoxycurcumin, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria in the stomach, reducing symptoms of upset stomach. These compounds work together to soothe and calm the digestive system, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Curcuma longa roots, a glass jar, a carrier oil like coconut oil, a cheesecloth, and a dark glass bottle.
Dry the Curcuma longa roots in the sun or using a food dehydrator for 2-3 days.
Grind 1 cup of dried Curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine 1/2 cup of the ground powder with 2 cups of carrier oil in the glass jar. Stir well and store in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through the cheesecloth into the dark glass bottle, discard the solids, and use the essential oil as needed for upset stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Pimpinella anisum essential oils contains a compound called anethole, which is responsible for its carminative and antispasmodic properties. This means it helps to prevent the formation of gas in the digestive system and relaxes the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing cramps and discomfort. The essential oil also contains estragole, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe an irritated stomach lining. Pimpinella anisum essential oils' antispasmodic properties can also help to reduce nausea and vomiting, making it a potential remedy for upset stomach. By relaxing the stomach and intestines and reducing inflammation, Pimpinella anisum essential oils can help to alleviate the symptoms of an upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Pimpinella anisum herbal essential oils for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Pimpinella anisum seeds and 2 cups of carrier oil, like jojoba or sweet almond oil.
Add 1/4 cup of vodka to a clean glass jar. Pour in the Pimpinella anisum seeds.
Leave the mixture to steep in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean jar.
Discard the seeds and store the Pimpinella anisum essential oil in a dark glass bottle, away from heat and light.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent an upset stomach. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, have soothing properties that can calm the stomach. They may also help reduce nausea and ease digestion. When you drink herbal tea, the active ingredients can reach your stomach and start to work, which might prevent an upset stomach from forming.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a good way to soothe an upset stomach, but having them every day might not be the best idea. Some herbs, like peppermint and chamomile, are gentle and safe, but others can be too strong or interact with other medications. It's best to drink them in moderation to avoid any potential issues.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with an upset stomach, but it depends on the type of tea and the person's sensitivity. Some people feel relief within 15 to 30 minutes after drinking, while others may notice improvement within a few hours. It's best to try different teas and see what works for you.
faq_time: If you have an upset stomach, try drinking herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. This can help soothe your stomach and give you a feeling of relief before meals. Avoid drinking it too close to bedtime, as it might make you feel more awake and uncomfortable when it's time to sleep.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for an upset stomach. Some herbs, like ginger and peppermint, may increase the risk of bleeding if taken with blood thinners. Other herbs, like chamomile, can slow down the movement of food through the stomach, making symptoms worse. Be cautious when combining herbal teas with medications.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and have an upset stomach, herbal teas might sound like a good idea. However, some herbal teas can be too strong for you and your baby. Peppermint and ginger teas are often safe, but be careful with teas like chamomile and licorice root, which can cause problems if not used right.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a good choice for an upset stomach while breastfeeding, but some need to be used carefully. Peppermint tea, for example, can help ease nausea, but it may cause gas and discomfort in some women. Ginger tea is often recommended, as it's gentle and can help soothe an upset stomach.
